core_mechanism: rare threshold-exceedance (extreme-value) statistics of a random field: the number density of regions where a fluctuating field crosses a fixed critical level, which is exponentially sensitive to the field's variance and to the shape (Gaussian vs. skewed/non-Gaussian) of the tail of its amplitude distribution
brief_reason: The abstract makes the abundance of clusters hinge on the mass-variance parameter and on whether the primordial density field is Gaussian or has a skewed chi^2_1 distribution, i.e. the count of collapsed objects is governed by how often the field exceeds a collapse threshold given the variance and tail shape.
